description_long: This kiosk features a striking Gothic architectural facade with two prominent pointed arches, each framing faded fresco-style murals. The surface is adorned with intricate stonework patterns above the arches, suggesting a historical or heritage-inspired design. The murals depict human figures in muted reds and greens, giving the structure an artistic and cultural presence. The sides and rear of the kiosk are plain and solid, with no visible openings or decorative elements. The roof is flat and unadorned, contrasting with the ornate front. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a small, freestanding heritage monument or information booth, likely intended as a decorative or commemorative urban feature.
description: A Gothic-style kiosk with two pointed arches and faded fresco murals on the front, featuring intricate stonework and a flat roof. The sides and rear are plain and solid, emphasizing the ornate front facade.
description_view_0: The front view displays two large pointed arches with faded fresco murals and detailed stonework above, forming the kiosk's most decorative side.
description_view_1: The left side view shows a narrow, plain surface with minimal detail, highlighting the depth and thickness of the structure.
description_view_2: The rear view is completely plain and solid black, with no visible features or decorations.
description_view_3: The right side view is also narrow and plain, mirroring the left side with no significant features.
attribute_roof_design: flat roof, solid, no overhangs or decorative features
attribute_architectural_style: Gothic Revival with pointed arches and decorative stonework
